vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1658: bug(pipeline): testStatus stuck 'pending' after rebase/force-push despite green CI — blocks merge, no reconciler

plan.title: Reconcile testStatus from green CI after rebase/force-push
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: When an approved PR's feature branch is rebased and force-pushed (e.g. to pick up a main fix), reviewStatus is already 'passed', so the review→test dispatch in setReviewStatusSync — which only fires on the pending→passed transition (review-status.ts:415-433) — never re-runs. testStatus stays 'pending', readyForMerge derives false (review-status.ts:259-272), and triggerMerge blocks with 'Cannot merge: review and tests have not passed yet' (workspaces.ts:4673-4683) — even though GitHub Actions CI (the same vitest+playwright suite) is fully green on the new HEAD. No deacon reconciler exists for pre-merge testStatus; the patrol reconciles merge status but not this.
narratives.Proposal: Add a default-on deacon reconciler modeled directly on reconcileClosedPrReadyForMerge (deacon.ts:3333): for each issue with reviewStatus='passed' && testStatus='pending' && a non-terminal mergeStatus && a GitHub PR URL, resolve the current PR HEAD and check whether the GitHub Actions CI check-runs are all green. If so, set testStatus='passed' with testNotes citing the CI run + SHA; readyForMerge then re-derives true automatically via the existing event-driven gate logic, unblocking merge across the whole UI/auto-merge/merge-train surface. Greenness is computed from check-runs only (not the self-reported panopticon/* commit-statuses, which are stuck/absent post-rebase and would make the signal circular). The reconciler never marks passed while CI is pending or failed; triggerMerge Step 3's full re-verification remains the authoritative quality gate.

autoDecisions.summary: Chose issue Option 1 (deacon reconciler) over Option 2 (triggerMerge gate accepts green CI) and Option 3 (re-dispatch the test role on force-push).
autoDecisions.rationale: Reconciling the root testStatus makes the derived readyForMerge flip true consistently across the dashboard 'Awaiting Merge' view, the auto-merge executor, the merge-train, and triggerMerge. Option 2 patches only the merge entry gate, leaving readyForMerge=false everywhere else (the issue still looks stuck) and adds an async GitHub call to the hot merge path. Option 3 re-runs the heavy, slow, gpt-5.5-context-wedge-prone review+test convoy the issue explicitly calls out as undesirable. Option 1 also matches the existing reconciler architecture exactly.

autoDecisions.summary: Greenness is determined from GitHub Actions check-runs, deliberately ignoring the self-reported panopticon/review and panopticon/test commit-statuses.
autoDecisions.rationale: After a rebase, panopticon/test is exactly the status that is stuck/absent on the new SHA, and an empty/pending combined commit-status would make getPullRequestState().checksPending permanently true — so reusing it naively would make the reconciler never fire. A check-runs-focused signal (≥1 successful CI run, none pending, none failed) breaks the circularity while still requiring real green CI.

autoDecisions.summary: Reconciler is unconditional / default-on (no new config flag), matching reconcileMergedButReviewing and reconcileClosedPrReadyForMerge.
autoDecisions.rationale: Premature testStatus=passed cannot cause a bad merge: triggerMerge Step 3 (runVerificationForIssue('merge-verify')) re-runs the full typecheck/lint/test suite on the rebased branch and aborts on failure. Adding a config flag would be speculative configurability the issue did not ask for. Operators retain the existing global deacon freeze as a kill switch.

autoDecisions.summary: The reconciler mutates ONLY testStatus (+ testNotes), never uatStatus/verificationStatus/reviewStatus/readyForMerge directly.
autoDecisions.rationale: readyForMerge is event-derived from the gate state, so flipping testStatus is sufficient. Leaving the other gates untouched preserves UAT and verification authority — an issue that still needs UAT stays not-ready even after testStatus flips, which is correct.

items.title: Add a check-runs-driven CI greenness signal for a commit SHA in github-app.ts

narrative.Action: Expose a reusable function in src/lib/github-app.ts that reports whether GitHub Actions CI is green on a given (owner, repo, sha), computed from the /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{sha}/check-runs endpoint ONLY. It must NOT depend on the combined commit-status (/status) endpoint, because that folds in the self-reported panopticon/review and panopticon/test contexts that are stuck/absent after a rebase (see continue.json hazard H1). The existing private getCommitCheckState already fetches check-runs; refactor/extend it (or add a sibling like getCiCheckRunsState) so the reconciler can ask a single, unambiguous question: 'are the CI check-runs on this exact SHA complete and all-successful?'. Return enough detail to distinguish green (≥1 success, 0 pending, 0 failed), not-yet (any pending/incomplete), and red (any failed). Empirically confirm against a real rebased PR that an absent/empty combined commit-status does not contaminate the signal before finalizing.

subItems.title: An exported function in github-app.ts returns a green verdict for a SHA only when there is ≥1 successful CI check-run AND zero pending/incomplete check-runs AND zero failed check-runs.

The greenness verdict is derived from the /commits/{sha}/check-runs endpoint only; it does NOT block on, or require, the panopticon/review or panopticon/test self-reported commit-statuses (a commit missing those statuses can still be judged green if its CI check-runs are green).

Any pending/incomplete check-run yields a 'not yet green' verdict (distinct from 'failed'); any failed/timed-out/cancelled check-run yields a 'red' verdict; a commit with zero CI check-runs is NOT green.

Existing callers of getCommitCheckState / getPullRequestState (checksPending/checksFailed behaviour and reconcileClosedPrReadyForMerge) are unchanged — typecheck, lint, and the existing github-app/PR-state tests stay green.

items.title: Add a deacon reconciler that flips stuck testStatus=pending → passed from green CI, and wire it into the patrol

narrative.Action: Add a reconciler function (e.g. reconcileTestStatusFromGreenCi(): Promise<string[]>) in src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts, modeled on reconcileClosedPrReadyForMerge (deacon.ts:3333). Guard on isGitHubAppConfigured(). For each entry in loadReviewStatuses() where reviewStatus==='passed' AND testStatus==='pending' (exactly) AND mergeStatus is non-terminal (pending/undefined/null — never merged/merging/queued/verifying) AND a parseable GitHub PR URL exists: resolve the current PR HEAD (getPullRequestState → headSha, the same primitive reconcileClosedPrReadyForMerge uses), skip if the PR is not OPEN or is merged, then evaluate the bead-1 CI greenness signal on that HEAD SHA. If green, call setReviewStatusSync(issueId, { testStatus: 'passed', testNotes: '<cite the CI run + short SHA>' }) — do NOT set readyForMerge or any other gate; it re-derives. Maintain a per-issue cooldown Map (set it on 'CI still pending' and on API error, like closedPrReadyReconcileCooldowns) so transient states don't burn the GitHub rate budget each patrol. Push a human-readable action string per reconciled issue and console.log it, matching the sibling reconcilers. Finally, wire the reconciler into the deacon patrol loop alongside the other reconcilers (deacon.ts ~5028-5041): call it, actions.push(...) the result, and addLog each action with state.patrolCycle.

subItems.title: For an issue with reviewStatus='passed', testStatus='pending', non-terminal mergeStatus, an OPEN unmerged PR, and green CI check-runs on the current HEAD, the reconciler sets testStatus='passed' with testNotes citing the CI run and the HEAD short SHA.
The reconciler never marks testStatus='passed' when CI on the current HEAD is pending/incomplete or failed/red; in those cases testStatus is left unchanged.

The candidate filter excludes testStatus values other than 'pending' (so a running 'testing' role, or already 'passed'/'skipped'/'failed', is never disturbed) and excludes terminal mergeStatus values (merged/merging/queued/verifying). The reconciler mutates only testStatus/testNotes — uatStatus, verificationStatus, reviewStatus, and readyForMerge are never written by it.

After the reconciler flips testStatus='passed', the existing event-driven derivation makes readyForMerge become true (assuming no other blocker/uat gate), so triggerMerge no longer returns 'Cannot merge: review and tests have not passed yet'.

The reconciler is guarded by isGitHubAppConfigured(), uses a per-issue cooldown on pending-CI and API-error outcomes to throttle GitHub calls, and returns string[] action descriptions consistent with the sibling reconcilers.

The reconciler is invoked once per deacon patrol cycle from the patrol loop, with its actions pushed into the patrol actions list and addLog'd, placed alongside reconcileMergedButReviewing / reconcileClosedPrReadyForMerge.

narrative.Action: Add a unit test (e.g. tests/unit/lib/cloister/test-status-reconciler.test.ts) for the new reconciler, mocking the GitHub PR/CI lookups and the review-status store so no network is required (follow the dependency-injection / module-mock pattern used by existing cloister reconciler tests such as merge-train-reconciler.test.ts and the github-app PR-state tests). Cover the happy path and every guard path so the reconciler can never mask a red build.

subItems.title: Test: reviewStatus=passed + testStatus=pending + non-terminal mergeStatus + OPEN PR + green CI check-runs ⇒ reconciler sets testStatus=passed (with testNotes) and reports one action.
Test: CI still pending ⇒ testStatus unchanged, no action; Test: CI failed/red ⇒ testStatus unchanged, no action (the reconciler never masks a red build).

Test: non-candidate states are untouched — testStatus='testing', testStatus already 'passed'/'skipped'/'failed', reviewStatus!='passed', and terminal mergeStatus (merged/merging/queued/verifying) each produce no mutation.

Test: greenness is judged on the CURRENT HEAD SHA (a stale/pre-rebase SHA is not used), and the per-issue cooldown suppresses repeat GitHub lookups within the throttle window on pending-CI / API-error outcomes.
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass with the new test included.
